prose:
**Formaldehyde frontier orbitals** — DFT, tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**, density-fit) in PySCF, **gas phase**, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied `.xyz` at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz` (4 atoms: O, C, H, H); this was a single point at the supplied geometry (not re-optimized). SCF converged in 8 cycles.

**Method used:** DFT B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF, RKS closed-shell), gas phase — exactly as requested.

**Headline result — HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.8696712 eV** (Koopmans-style orbital gap, not an excitation energy).

Frontier orbital energies:
- HOMO (index 7) = −7.6523067 eV
- LUMO (index 8) = −1.7826355 eV
- Gap = 5.8696712 eV
- Nearby: HOMO−1 = −11.002498 eV, HOMO−2 = −12.486938 eV; LUMO+1 = +1.3920007 eV, LUMO+2 = +2.8396981 eV

Koopmans-derived descriptors (from these orbital energies): vertical IP = 7.6523067 eV, vertical EA = 1.7826355 eV, electronegativity = 4.7174711 eV, chemical hardness = 2.9348356 eV, chemical softness = 0.34073459 eV⁻¹, electrophilicity index = 3.7914447 eV.

Total electronic energy: −114.55325 Hartree (−3117.1528 eV). Note this energy origin is B3LYP/def2-TZVP electronic energy (bare nuclei + electrons) and is only comparable to other same-method, same-basis values.

**Convergence & warnings:** SCF converged (`scf_converged: true`, 8 cycles). The result JSON contains **no `warnings` array / no warnings reported** — clean run. Integrity engine verdict: **trustworthy = true**, status "ok" (all checks passed: energy_finite ok, scf_converged ok, schema_header ok, schema_headline ok).

**Reproducibility:** `cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard <formaldehyde.xyz>`. Density fitting (RI) is on by default in chemkit. No experimental/literature comparison is offered; ask if you want one and I will verify a source first.
